subject: Computer Networking
title: HTTP/3: The Future of the Web
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: HTTP/3: The Future of the Web.

hook.text: HTTP/1 was simple. HTTP/2 added multiplexing. HTTP/3 throws away TCP entirely and starts fresh with QUIC.

buildup.text: HTTP/3 keeps the same semantics — GET, POST, headers, status codes — but the transport layer is completely new.

discovery.text: Because QUIC handles encryption natively, HTTP/3 connections set up faster than HTTP/2 over TLS.

twist.text: HTTP/3 handles network switches gracefully. Move from Wi-Fi to cellular? The connection survives seamlessly.

climax.text: Google, Cloudflare, and Facebook already serve traffic over HTTP/3. Adoption is accelerating rapidly.

punchline.text: HTTP/3 is HTTP rebuilt on a faster foundation.

quiz.question: What transport protocol does HTTP/3 use instead of TCP?
options: A new version of TCP called TCP/2
